description:
dnsmasq is a lightweight tool that provides DNS caching, DNS forwarding and DHCP (Dynamic Host Configuration Protocol) services.
The dnsmasq package can be removed with the following command: 
$ sudo dnf remove dnsmasq
rationale:
Unless a system is specifically designated to act as a DNS
caching, DNS forwarding and/or DHCP server, it is recommended that the
package be removed to reduce the potential attack surface.
ocil:
Run the following command to determine if the dnsmasq package is installed: 
$ rpm -q dnsmasq
title: Uninstall dnsmasq Package
